title: The Adventures of Toto – Easy LAQs

questions.question: How did Grandfather get Toto? Describe the incident.
questions.answer: Grandfather bought Toto from a tonga-driver for five rupees. The monkey was tied to a feeding trough and looked unhappy. Grandfather felt sorry for him and decided to add him to his private zoo.

questions.question: Describe Toto’s appearance.
questions.answer: Toto was a small and pretty monkey with bright mischievous eyes and pearly white teeth. His tail acted like a third hand and helped him hold things easily.

questions.question: Why was Toto kept secretly in the house at first?
questions.answer: Toto was kept secretly because Grandmother did not like new animals in the house. Grandfather and the narrator decided to hide Toto until she was in a good mood.

questions.question: What mischief did Toto do in the closet?
questions.answer: Toto pulled out the peg from the wall, tore the wallpaper, and shredded the narrator’s school blazer. The entire room was left in a mess.

questions.question: Describe Grandfather’s private zoo.
questions.answer: Grandfather’s private zoo had many animals such as rabbits, a tortoise, a squirrel, and a goat. Toto was later added to this collection.

questions.question: Why did Grandfather take Toto to Saharanpur?
questions.answer: Grandfather had to go to Saharanpur to collect his pension. Since Toto could not be left behind, he decided to take him along.

questions.question: How was Toto carried during the train journey?
questions.answer: Toto was kept in a big black canvas kit-bag with some straw at the bottom. The bag was tied so that Toto could not escape.

questions.question: Describe the incident with the ticket collector.
questions.answer: When Toto stuck his head out of the bag, the ticket collector saw him and said he was a dog. Grandfather had to pay three rupees as fare.

questions.question: What happened when Grandfather showed the tortoise to the ticket collector?
questions.answer: Grandfather showed his tortoise and asked if he should pay for it. The ticket collector replied that there was no charge because it was not a dog.

questions.question: Who was Nana and how did Toto trouble her?
questions.answer: Nana was the family donkey. Toto bit her ears with his sharp teeth and frightened her.

questions.question: Describe Toto’s bathing habit.
questions.answer: Toto enjoyed warm baths. He tested the water with his hand and slowly entered it until he was neck-deep.

questions.question: How did Toto dry himself after bathing?
questions.answer: After bathing, Toto ran to the kitchen fire to dry himself.

questions.question: Explain the kettle incident.
questions.answer: Toto jumped into a kettle of warm water placed on the fire. As the water became hot, he kept hopping until Grandmother saved him.

questions.question: Describe the lunch-time incident involving Toto.
questions.answer: Toto jumped onto the dining table and started eating the dish of pullao. When people tried to stop him, he threw plates and escaped.

questions.question: Where did Toto go after stealing the dish of pullao?
questions.answer: He ran to a jackfruit tree and sat on a branch while eating the rice.

questions.question: What did Toto do after finishing the rice?
questions.answer: After finishing the rice, Toto threw the dish down from the tree and broke it.

questions.question: Why was Toto considered troublesome?
questions.answer: Toto destroyed many things such as clothes, dishes, and wallpaper. His mischief caused trouble for the family.

questions.question: Why did the family decide to sell Toto?
questions.answer: The family sold Toto because his mischief caused too much damage that they could not afford.

questions.question: For how much was Toto sold?
questions.answer: Toto was sold back to the tonga-driver for three rupees.

questions.question: What kind of animal was Toto?
questions.answer: Toto was a playful and mischievous monkey.

questions.question: How does the author describe Toto’s intelligence?
questions.answer: The author shows Toto’s intelligence through his clever actions and ability to imitate humans.

questions.question: Why is the story humorous?
questions.answer: The story is humorous because of Toto’s funny and mischievous actions.

questions.question: What lesson do we learn from the story?
questions.answer: The story teaches that wild animals may not be suitable as pets.

questions.question: What role does Grandfather play in the story?
questions.answer: Grandfather is the owner of Toto and loves animals. His affection for animals leads him to keep Toto despite his mischief.

questions.question: Summarise the story 'The Adventures of Toto'.
questions.answer: The story tells about a mischievous monkey named Toto bought by Grandfather. Toto causes many funny incidents in the house. Finally, the family sells him back because they cannot manage his mischief.
